stub: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background.
personalization: She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership.
substantive: She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week.
justification: In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole.
responsiveness: Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I always listen to voters’ views, because at the end of the day they get to choose whether to reelect me or not.”
text: She is a Labour MP who comes from a white working class background. She is from your constituency. She frequently speaks out and votes against her party leadership. She opposes increasing the basic Universal Credit grant by £20 per week. In political debates, she regularly uses arguments that appeal to society as a whole. Asked recently about the role and duties of politicians, she said: “I always listen to voters’ views, because at the end of the day they get to choose whether to reelect me or not.”
